questions.question: Does the welcome page make clear who this is for?
Is 'OGX Off-Grid Simulator' obvious enough?
Would a beginner know what to do first?
Are 7 climate options overwhelming?
Are the emojis helpful or distracting?
Does each blurb actually help someone pick?
Are the 7 dwelling types distinct enough?
Is 'Catamaran/Boat' relevant for land-based users?
Is the info enough to make a choice?
Does $3k/$8k/$20k make sense as starting budgets?
Is the price tier (x0.5/x1/x2) intuitive?
Is 'Full DIY' vs 'Start from preset' clear?
After clicking begin, is the console layout clear?
Are the 5 dashboards ordered logically?
Is the budget tracker visible and understandable?
Does the SVG scene add value or confusion?
Is the stress-test button obvious?
Does the intro modal actually teach something useful?
Are the step names clear? (Generate, Panels, Battery...)
Are trade-offs between options visible?
Do the tags (DIY pick, risk, safety) help?
Are water source options clear for a beginner?
Does the priority feel right (gathering > moving > storing > filtering)?
Is 'grow outside' vs 'controlled' vs 'preserve' logical?
Are mushrooms, aquaponics too niche for a first build?
Does 'deter > harden > fire > community' make sense as a priority order?
Is Starlink pricing accurate? Does the power note make sense?
Are mystery cards surprising enough?
Do the lessons actually teach something?
Is 'quiet year' satisfying or anti-climactic?
Dark theme: readable?
Font choice: Oswald/JetBrains Mono/Inter appropriate?
Responsive: does grid layout work on mobile?
Color coding: do accent colors (gold/blue/green/red/teal) make sense?
